title: The Macro Minute: Will tax cuts bail out the slowing US economy?

happy Friday out there team 42 your
Skipper here at Darius Dell to present
our macro minute for Friday March 28
2025 as always we'll start with the
executive summary from today's leadoff
warning note if you would like to review
the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of Market risk
need to be a client of 42 macro so
today's key macro question is will tax
cuts bail out the slowing US economy our
answers are eventually but likely not on
the accelerated timeline that many
investors are hoping for consistent with
the kitchen sinking thesis that we
authored last fall we believe the US
economy is headed for at least a two to
three quter rough patch based on the
cumulative impact of steepening Base
effects historically elevated policy
uncertainty tariff implementation
slowing labor Supply growth due to the
change in Border policy and Doge budget
cuts this outcome is not yet reflected
in consensus sales earnings or GDP
estimates there needs to be a meaningful
expansion of the tax cuts and job acts
to have a meaningfully positive impulse
from the legisl a positive fiscal
impulse from the
legislation that is especially true
considering several key economic
constraints that deregulation will
amarate but not likely eliminate
ultimately Congress has two options
number one adopt a current policy
Baseline to turbocharge the US economy
with a permanent extension and
significant expansion of the Trump tax
cuts and risk blowing up the bond market
in the process or adopt a current law
basine which is to accept the harsh
reality of a limited positive fiscal
impulse from a mere temporary extension
of the tcja with maybe a few added
sweeteners here and there like no tax on
tips or
overtime if president Trump prefers
option number one what better time to
pursue that agenda than during a
self-imposed kitchen sinking of the US
economy it's hard to blow up the bond
market during an economic downturn when
most investors are inclined to rotate
out of risk assets into the perceived
safety of Treasury bonds transition to
our 42 micro dashboard as always a wrap
up the question from our community uh
